seoTitle: Phased product rebuild: without big bang or external tech lead | Luc Rousseau
seoDescription: Stack change or product rebuild: feasibility, phasing, go/no-go, documented architecture — without running the dev team's day to day.

hero.title: Rebuild a product without stopping everything — and without owning the team chair
hero.lede:
Pressure builds: new stack, headless, Laravel, new front — or « we can't live with this system anymore ». A full rewrite feels like the only way out, but stop-the-world cost and overrun risk are real.

I work on decision, phasing, and product architecture: feasibility, milestones, boundaries, migration order — without becoming tech lead or managing your devs daily. Your team (or agency) ships; I keep strategic thread and system coherence.

blocks.title: The big bang that drifts
blocks.lede: Rebuild often means 12–24 months of fog, two systems in parallel, and a team executing without a clear map. Without someone holding why and in what order, sprints pile up until budget and trust are gone.

items.title: Feasibility and go/no-go
items.content: Before major spend: real constraints, stack options, risks, whether rebuild is the right answer — or targeted extraction is enough.

items.title: Phasing with deliverables
items.content: Slices that ship value (like progressive migration to a block-based stack) — not an 18-month tunnel with nothing to show.

items.title: Architecture and boundaries
items.content: APIs, modules, contracts, debt to tackle first — documented so devs and vendors build in the same mental model.

items.title: Coherence oversight, not people management
items.content: Targeted reviews on roadmap ↔ architecture ↔ prod alignment — without running stand-up for you or micro-managing tasks.

blocks.title: Internal rebuild vs external product-system partner
blocks.intro: Each engagement is scoped in written phases.

rows.internalOnly: Often lost in execution; why drifts.
rows.phasedPartner: Go/no-go, sequence, success criteria held externally.
Slices with shipped value; adjust between phases.
No lead replacement: system coherence and product-tech trade-offs.

items.title: Will you become our tech lead?
items.content: No. Your devs or agency keep operational rhythm. I clarify system and sequence; I don't manage people or daily tickets.

items.title: We can't afford to slow down to plan
items.content: Two to four weeks of framing often avoids months of rework. Phase one can be exactly that — deliverable: plan, target architecture, migration order.

items.title: What after the rebuild?
items.content: Documented handover; optional fractional follow-up to keep coherence without a permanent role — same phased logic as elsewhere on the site.

blocks.label: Discuss your product rebuild
blocks.teaser: Pressure to rewrite everything? One call to see if phased feasibility and clear architecture beat a tunnel without a map.
